title: crataegus douglasii var. rivularis
intro: Crataegus douglasii var. rivularis, also known as the 'River Snow' hawthorn, is a variety of the Douglas hawthorn shrub or small tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidin, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Crataegus douglasii var. rivularis include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which can be used to promote cardiovascular health and well-being.
